name: Zippy's Restaurant
tagline: Hawaii's diner chain that every local grew up on — chili and zip packs are a way of life.
mustOrder: Zippy's Chili — the iconic thick, beefy chili that's been the same since 1966
Zip Pack — fried chicken, chili, rice, and mac salad in one legendary combo
Napoleon Bakery cream puffs — the grab-and-go bakery case is dangerously good
parking: Most locations have their own lot. King Street location has a parking lot behind the building.
talkStory: Founded in 1966 by Francis Higa and Charles Toguchi, Zippy's is Hawaii's most beloved diner chain with 20+ locations across Oahu. The chili recipe is a legit state secret — they've never revealed it in nearly 60 years. Every local kid grew up eating here after practice, after prom, at 2am. The Zip Pack (fried chicken, chili, rice, mac salad) is an institution. Napoleon Bakery inside sells cakes and cream puffs for every birthday party on the island. It's not fancy, but for locals it's home.
